nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: Sakalava rail
nodes.summary: Sakalava rail（Sakalava rail，学名 Zapornia olivieri），分类上属于鹤形目，并归入秧鸡科，在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，常见栖息地包括湿地、沼泽，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、人类活动扰动。

nodes.name: 秧鸡科
nodes.summary: 秧鸡科（Rallidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联103种鸟类，如Tsingy forest rail、Madagascar forest rail、Chestnut forest rail等103种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鹤形目
nodes.summary: 鹤形目（Gruiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联126种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、灰冠鹤、Grey-winged trumpeter等126种鸟类。

nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: This bird is small with brown upperpart feathers, grey underparts, a yellow bill and red legs. The habitat of this rail species is freshwater marshes of reed Phragmites mauritianus. It is classified as Endangered and is threatened by habitat loss due to the destruction of wetlands in Madagascar

It is classified as Endangered and is threatened by habitat loss due to the destruction of wetlands in Madagascar

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: This bird is small with brown upperpart feathers, grey underparts, a yellow bill and red legs. The habitat of this rail species is freshwater marshes of reed Phragmites mauritianus. It is classified as Endangered and is threatened by habitat loss due to the destruction of wetlands in Madagascar

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Habitat loss is a major threat to this rail species when the shores of marshes where Sakalava rails nest are converted into rice fields. Also, human disturbance degrades the habitat of Sakalava rails when local populations burn and collect Phragmites reeds. Indeed, the surface area occupied by reeds has decreased by 80% between 1949 and 2008 due to increased turbidity from erosion. These wetlands of western Madagascar are also home to five other threatened bird species: Madagascar fish eagle (Haliaeetus vociferoides), Madagascar heron (Ardea humbloti), Madagascar sacred ibis (Threskiornis bernieri), Madagascar teal (Anas bernieri) and the Madagascar plover (Charadrius thoracicus).{{Cite journal|last1=Young|first1=H
